name: Along the Salat by Seix
name_original: Le long du Salat par Seix
region_intro: Hiking around Ustou offers diverse natural landscapes within the Pyrénées Ariégeoises Regional Natural Park. The region is characterized by impressive glacial cirques like the Cirque de Cagateille and Cirque d'Anglade, alongside prominent mountain peaks and scenic passes. Hikers can explore the Alet valley, dense forests, and discover numerous waterfalls, such as the Ars Waterfalls, and mountain lakes. This varied terrain provides a range of hiking experiences from valley walks to challenging mountain ascents. Best hiking trails around Ustou The most popular hiking route is Cirque de Cagateille – loop via the étang de la Hillette and the étang d'Alet – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises, a difficult 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail that takes 6 hours 4 minutes to complete, offering views of glacial lakes within the regional park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cirque de Cagateille – Forest path loop from La Peyre, a moderate 2.3 miles (3.6 km) path. This route provides a shorter, accessible forest experience around the Cirque de Cagateille. Local hikers also love the Cirque de Cagateille – View of the Ustou Valley loop from La Peyre, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail leading through the Ustou Valley, often completed in about 4 hours 29 minutes. Hiking around Ustou is defined by dramatic glacial cirques, high mountain peaks, and lush river valleys with waterfalls. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate forest paths to difficult multi-hour ascents. The routes in Ustou are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Ustou's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Ustou?
A: Ustou, nestled within the Pyrénées Ariégeoises Regional Natural Park, offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll encounter impressive glacial cirques like the Cirque de Cagateille and Cirque d'Anglade, alongside prominent mountain peaks and scenic passes. The Alet valley features lush forests and river-based paths, while higher elevations lead to waterfalls and mountain lakes. Expect everything from gentle valley walks to challenging multi-hour ascents with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there easy hiking options for beginners or families in Ustou?
A: Yes, Ustou offers a variety of trails suitable for different skill levels. For an easier experience, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20527537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cirque de Cagateille – Forest path loop from La Peyre&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 2.3-mile (3.6 km) path provides an accessible forest experience around the Cirque de Cagateille, ideal for families or those seeking a less strenuous walk.

Q: What are some challenging hikes in the Ustou area?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Ustou has several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949906729/cirque-de-cagateille-loop-via-the-etang-de-la-hillette-and-the-etang-d-alet-parc-naturel-regional-des-pyrenees-ariegeoises" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cirque de Cagateille – loop via the étang de la Hillette and the étang d'Alet&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 7.3-mile (11.7 km) trail that takes over 6 hours to complete, offering views of glacial lakes. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40352674" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Cascade d'Ars – Ars Waterfall (Middle) loop from Guzet-Neige Prat Mataou&lt;/a&gt;, a long 18-mile (29 km) route with significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find hikes that feature waterfalls in Ustou?
A: Absolutely. Ustou is known for its beautiful waterfalls. The most prominent are the Ars Waterfalls, which can be reached via several trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38225252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail to the Ars Waterfalls – View of Aulus-les-Bains loop from Pic de Crespies&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 13.3-mile (21.4 km) route that leads to these impressive cascades.

Q: Are there any mountain lakes accessible by hiking in Ustou?
A: Yes, the Ustou region boasts numerous picturesque mountain lakes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949906729/cirque-de-cagateille-loop-via-the-etang-de-la-hillette-and-the-etang-d-alet-parc-naturel-regional-des-pyrenees-ariegeoises" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cirque de Cagateille – loop via the étang de la Hillette and the étang d'Alet&lt;/a&gt; offers views of glacial lakes within the regional park. Other notable lakes in the wider area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5160706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estats Pond&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3106229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estany de Romedo de Dalt&lt;/a&gt;, which are significant natural features in the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking trails in Ustou?
A: Yes, Ustou offers options for families. While some routes are challenging, there are easier paths suitable for children and less experienced hikers. Look for shorter, less steep loops, particularly those in forested areas or along the valley floor, which provide a pleasant outdoor experience without excessive difficulty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20527537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cirque de Cagateille – Forest path loop from La Peyre&lt;/a&gt; is a good example of a moderate, shorter option.

Q: Is Ustou a dog-friendly hiking destination?
A: Many trails in the Pyrénées Ariégeoises Regional Natural Park, including those around Ustou, are generally dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. However, it's always advisable to check specific trail regulations, particularly in protected areas or near grazing livestock. Ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you carry waste bags to maintain the natural environment.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Ustou?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular way to explore Ustou's landscapes. Many trails are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949906729/cirque-de-cagateille-loop-via-the-etang-de-la-hillette-and-the-etang-d-alet-parc-naturel-regional-des-pyrenees-ariegeoises" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cirque de Cagateille – loop via the étang de la Hillette and the étang d'Alet&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5347997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cirque de Cagateille – View of the Ustou Valley loop from La Peyre&lt;/a&gt;, both offering varied scenery on a return path.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ustou?
A: The best time for hiking in Ustou is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally milder, trails are clear of snow at lower to mid-altitudes, and the flora is vibrant. Summer offers the warmest temperatures, while spring and autumn provide beautiful colors and fewer crowds. Higher mountain routes may still have snow well into early summer.

Q: Is parking available at trailheads in Ustou?
A: Yes, parking is generally available at or near popular trailheads in Ustou and the surrounding areas. As part of the Pyrénées Ariégeoises Regional Natural Park, efforts are made to provide access for visitors. However, during peak season, spaces can fill up quickly, especially for well-known routes like those leading to the Cirque de Cagateille or Ars Waterfalls. Arriving early is recommended.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Ustou?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Ustou, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 250 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic glacial cirques, the stunning Ars Waterfalls, and the serene mountain lakes. The diverse landscapes, from lush valley forests to high mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Agnès Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lers Pass&lt;/a&gt;, are frequently highlighted as key attractions.

Q: Are there any interesting natural monuments or viewpoints around Ustou?
A: Ustou is rich in natural monuments and offers spectacular viewpoints. Beyond the impressive glacial cirques, you can find prominent peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/480323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pica d'Estats&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2830930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcalm Peak&lt;/a&gt;, which provide breathtaking panoramic views. The region also features scenic mountain passes such as Col d'Agnes and Port de Lers, known for their beauty and vistas.
tips: Shaded and quiet road, away from traffic, which runs along the Salat before entering the Col de la Pause.

This superb village is located at the foot of the mountains. In summer you will find some activities. The rest of the time, it is a quiet village. The place is perfect for having a coffee, and it's a good starting point for some nice walks.
